title: lippia bromleyana
intro: Lippia bromleyana, also known as African bush tea or wild tea, is a shrub-like plant native to tropical Africa, belonging to the verbena family (Verbenaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, offering health benefits like reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. The leaves can be prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a herbal remedy, or they can be dried and used as a herbal supplement.
